candidates.notes: H3 (revision helps) PASS: +5.3% average improvement with revision, +2.7% for standard draft, +8.0% for aggressive draft. H4 (calibration correction > raw entropy) technically PASS but calibration adds ZERO improvement over raw entropy (both 41% for standard, both 33% for aggressive). This suggests the isotonic calibration doesn't change the entropy-based ranking meaningfully. The calibration study itself (H1, H2) remains valid — DLMs ARE miscalibrated — but the correction doesn't improve revision target selection in practice. Aggressive draft is strongly harmful (-14% vs standard with no revision). Random revision helps (+2% standard, +6% aggressive) but entropy-based is better (+3% standard, +9% aggressive). Key tokens_changed insight: entropy targets 4.8 tokens/sample vs random 0.3 — entropy finds the truly fragile tokens.

Standard baselines established. 64-step accuracy (38%) is the best, beating both 32 (29%) and 128 (33%). Non-monotonic: 128-step is WORSE than 64-step, suggesting over-denoising. DNB-84 baseline should be approximately 38%. CARD with raw-entropy revision achieves 41% at 68 NFE, which already beats the DNB-84 estimate. The non-monotonicity at 128 steps further supports revision-based approaches over simply adding more steps.

H6 FALSIFIED: Temperature annealing uniformly degrades performance. All 3 schedules (linear 36%, quadratic 35%, cosine 34%) are worse than isothermal (41%). The -5% degradation is substantial and consistent. High temperature early disrupts confidence-based unmasking quality; committed errors propagate. The cosine unmasking schedule already provides implicit explore-exploit tradeoff. CARD should use isothermal T=1.0. This simplifies the method (fewer hyperparameters) and is a useful negative result for the paper.

H5 STRONG PASS: CARD-84 (43%) beats DNB-84 (32%) by +11%, far exceeding the 1% threshold. Critical finding: standard denoising is strongly non-monotonic — more steps HURTS (84 steps=32%, 96 steps=30%, vs 64 steps=38%). This means the DNB baseline is actually weaker than expected, but CARD still beats Standard-64 by +5%. CARD-84 achieves 43% at only 71 actual NFE (not 84 as budgeted, because revision steps are cheaper than full forward passes on the complete sequence). Both CARD variants are on the Pareto frontier. Per-sample: CARD flips 17 samples correct that DNB misses, while losing only 6. The revision mechanism targets ~6 tokens/sample and changes ~5.9 of them, confirming meaningful corrections. PROCEED TO FULL experiments with high confidence.
